chapter_title: Salt; sulphur; earths and stone; plastering materials, lime and cement
inclusions: Salt (table salt, denatured salt, rock salt, pure sodium chloride in aqueous solution or with anti-caking agents); sea water
Unroasted iron pyrites (FeS2)
Sulphur of all kinds except sublimed, precipitated and colloidal sulphur
Natural graphite (powder, flakes or other forms)
Natural sands of all kinds whether or not coloured — except metal-bearing sands of Chapter 26 (silica sands, quartz sands, other)
Quartz (other than natural sands); quartzite in blocks or slabs of rectangular shape
Kaolin and other kaolinic clays, whether or not calcined
Other clays (excl. expanded clays of 68.06): bentonite, fire-clay, fuller's earth, other clays; andalusite, kyanite, sillimanite; mullite; chamotte or dinas earths
Chalk
Natural calcium phosphates, natural aluminium calcium phosphates and phosphatic chalk (including apatite)
Natural barium sulphate (barytes); natural barium carbonate (witherite), whether or not calcined
Siliceous fossil meals (kieselguhr, tripolite, diatomite) and similar siliceous earths of apparent specific gravity of 1 or less, whether or not calcined
Pumice stone; emery; natural corundum, natural garnet and other natural abrasives, whether or not heat-treated
Slate in blocks or slabs of rectangular shape
Marble, travertine, ecaussine and other calcareous monumental/building stone (SG ≥ 2.5) and alabaster — crude, roughly trimmed or merely cut into rectangular blocks/slabs
Granite, porphyry, basalt, sandstone and other monumental/building stone — crude, roughly trimmed or merely cut into rectangular blocks/slabs
Pebbles, gravel, broken or crushed stone for concrete/road/railway ballast; macadam of slag; tarred macadam; granules, chippings and powder of stones of heading 25.15 or 25.16
Dolomite whether or not calcined or sintered, in blocks/slabs or other forms
Natural magnesium carbonate (magnesite); fused magnesia; dead-burned (sintered) magnesia; other magnesium oxide
Gypsum; anhydrite; plasters of calcined gypsum or calcium sulphate (including dental plasters)
Limestone flux; limestone and other calcareous stone for manufacture of lime or cement
Quicklime, slaked lime and hydraulic lime (excluding calcium oxide and hydroxide of heading 28.25)
Portland cement, aluminous cement, slag cement, supersulphate cement and similar hydraulic cements, whether or not coloured or in the form of clinkers
Asbestos (crocidolite and other)
Mica including splittings; mica waste (crude, sheets, powder)
Natural steatite (not crushed/powdered or crushed/powdered); talc
Natural borates and concentrates (whether or not calcined); natural boric acid containing not more than 85% of H3BO3 on dry weight
Feldspar; leucite; nepheline and nepheline syenite; fluorspar
Mineral substances not elsewhere specified or included: vermiculite, perlite, chlorites (unexpanded); natural magnesium sulphates (kieserite, epsomite); micronized zircon sand; other

exclusions.condition: Sublimed sulphur, precipitated sulphur or colloidal sulphur
Earth colours containing 70% or more by weight of combined iron evaluated as Fe2O3
Medicaments or other products of Chapter 30
Setts, curbstones or flagstones (worked paving stone)
Roofing, facing or damp course slates (worked slate)

exclusions.action: Redirect to Heading 71.02 (precious stones) or 71.03 (semi-precious stones)

exclusions.condition: Cultured crystals (other than optical elements) weighing not less than 2.5g each of sodium chloride or magnesium oxide
exclusions.action: Redirect to Heading 38.24; optical elements of NaCl or MgO → Heading 90.01

exclusions.condition: Writing or drawing chalks or tailors' chalks

exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 26 (ores, slag and ash)

exclusions.condition: Calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide (chemically pure)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Heading 28.25 — Note: heading 25.22 covers commercial-grade quicklime and slaked lime only, not pure calcium oxide/hydroxide

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Maximum processing allowed in Chapter 25
classification_rules.description: Chapter 25 headings cover only products in the CRUDE STATE or that have been: washed (even with chemicals that eliminate impurities without changing structure), crushed, ground, powdered, levigated, sifted, screened, concentrated by flotation, magnetic separation, or other MECHANICAL/PHYSICAL processes (EXCEPT crystallisation). Products that have been ROASTED, CALCINED (except where explicitly allowed per heading), obtained by MIXING, or subjected to FURTHER processing → excluded from Chapter 25 (go to Ch.26, 28, 38, or 68 depending on the product). Anti-dusting agents may be added provided they do not render the product suitable for specific rather than general use.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Heading 25.17 has absolute priority within Chapter 25
classification_rules.description: Any product classifiable in heading 25.17 AND any other heading of Chapter 25 MUST be classified in heading 25.17. This means pebbles, gravel, crushed/broken stone (for concrete aggregates, road metalling, ballast), macadam, tarred macadam, or granules/chippings/powder from stones of 25.15/25.16 always take priority as 25.17.

classification_rules.rule: Stone processing level determines Chapter 25 vs Chapter 68
classification_rules.description: Key distinction: Chapter 25 = crude, roughly trimmed, OR merely cut into rectangular blocks/slabs. Chapter 68 (worked stone) = further worked beyond rectangular cutting, e.g. polished, shaped, profiled, carved, etc. Marble merely cut into slabs → 25.15; marble polished or shaped into tiles/monuments → 68.02. This applies to marble, granite, sandstone, slate, etc.

classification_rules.rule: Sea water vs. other water classification
classification_rules.description: Sea water → heading 25.01. Natural mineral water (plain, no sugar/flavour) → heading 22.01. Distilled or conductivity water (high purity industrial) → heading 28.53. Sea water in Chapter 25, not Chapter 22 — this is a key distinction.

Heading 25.01 sub-classifies by NaCl content on dry basis: table salt → 2501.00.10; unprocessed rock salt → 2501.00.20; sea water → 2501.00.50; NaCl 60–97% (iodised) → 2501.00.91; NaCl ≥97% → 2501.00.93; other → 2501.00.99.

classification_rules.rule: Lime classification: quicklime vs. slaked lime vs. calcium oxide (28.25)
classification_rules.description: Commercial-grade lime products: quicklime (cao độ CaO, commercial) → 2522.10; slaked lime (Ca(OH)2, commercial) → 2522.20; hydraulic lime → 2522.30. HOWEVER, chemically pure calcium oxide (CaO) or calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) → 28.25, not 25.22.

Cement clinkers → 2523.10. Portland cement: white → 2523.21; coloured → 2523.29.10; ordinary (grey) → 2523.29.90. Aluminous cement → 2523.30. Other hydraulic cements (slag cement, supersulphate, etc.) → 2523.90.

classification_rules.rule: Fluorspar classification by calcium fluoride content (25.29)
classification_rules.description: Fluorspar sub-classified by CaF2 content: ≤97% CaF2 → heading 2529.21; >97% CaF2 → heading 2529.22. Higher purity fluorspar (>97%) is distinct from lower purity.
